messages.content: You are BioGPT, an expert compiler architect. Translate the provided natural language biological protocol into valid, deterministic BioScript code for execution on a microfluidic biochip.

Perform a colony PCR for screening assemblies. First, dispense 50 units of water into a lysis well and 150 units of LB antibiotic medium into a copy well. Dispense an E. coli colony and split it into two parts. Mix one part with the water to create the lysis well, and mix the other part with the LB medium to create the copy well. Dispense 10 units of PCR mastermix and mix it into the lysis well to create the PCR reaction. Perform an initial denaturation by heating the PCR reaction at 95 degrees C for 10 minutes. Repeat the following thermal cycle 30 times: heat at 95 degrees C for 30 seconds, anneal at 64 degrees C for 15 seconds, and extend at 72 degrees C for 1 minute. Follow with a final extension at 72 degrees C for 10 minutes. Dispense 2 units of gel loading buffer and mix it with the PCR reaction to create the gel sample. Detect fluorescence on the gel sample. If a band is detected (fluorescence > 0), rescue the clone by dispensing 100 units of TY rich media, mixing it with the copy well to create a rescue culture, and heating it at 37 degrees C for 24 hours before disposal. If no band is detected, dispose of the copy well. Finally, dispose of the gel sample.

module fluorescence

manifest h2o
manifest lb_antibiotic
manifest e_coli_colony
manifest pcr_mastermix
manifest loading_buffer
manifest ty_media

instructions:

water = dispense 50 units of h2o
lb_media = dispense 150 units of lb_antibiotic
colony = dispense e_coli_colony

colony_parts = split colony into 2

lysis_well = mix water with colony_parts[0]
copy_well = mix lb_media with colony_parts[1]

mm_buffer = dispense 10 units of pcr_mastermix
pcr_reaction = mix lysis_well with mm_buffer

heat pcr_reaction at 95c for 10m

repeat 30 times {
    heat pcr_reaction at 95c for 30s
    heat pcr_reaction at 64c for 15s
    heat pcr_reaction at 72c for 1m
}

heat pcr_reaction at 72c for 10m

gel_dye = dispense 2 units of loading_buffer
gel_sample = mix pcr_reaction with gel_dye

band_detected = detect fluorescence on gel_sample

if (band_detected > 0) {
    rich_media = dispense 100 units of ty_media
    rescue_culture = mix copy_well with rich_media
    heat rescue_culture at 37c for 24h
    dispose rescue_culture
} else {
    dispose copy_well
}

dispose gel_sample

metadata.notes: IR and DAG Validated. Perfectly matches the OpenWetWare PrbbBB Colony PCR screening protocol. Uses dynamic conditional logic based on a sensor reading (fluorescence) to determine biological rescue routing.
